title: Phloremirlla Tenk

{{Quote|This isn't supposed to happen. This isn't ''happening''. It ''can't'' be happening.|Phloremirlla Tenk, after being shot|<ref name="Shatterpoint (novel)">[[Shatterpoint (novel)|''Shatterpoint'']]</ref>}}
'''Phloremirlla Tenk''', ubiquitously known simply as '''Flor''', was a [[Republic Intelligence/Legends|Republic Intelligence]] agent. She was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|woman]] with a red face, roughly [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu's]] age. She joined Republic Intelligence in [[55 BBY/Legends|55 BBY]]. By [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], she held the rank of [[RS-17]] and was planetary chief of Republic Intelligence on [[Haruun Kal/Legends|Haruun Kal]].

She was stationed out of [[Pelek Baw/Legends|Pelek Baw]], the capital city of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Her cover was head of the [[Highland Green Washeteria]]; five of her 37 employees were also Intelligence agents. Flor became friends with [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Lorz Geptun]], head of the Pelek Baw militia. During the early stages of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] she became a double agent, and secretly worked for the [[Balawai]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].

When [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] traveled to Haruun Kal six months after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] to look for the rogue Master [[Depa Billaba/Legends|Depa Billaba]], he was instructed to contact Tenk, who would bring him to an upcountry team for the journey to Billaba and her band of [[Korun/Legends|Korunnai]] rebels. While leading Windu to the team, however, Tenk was hit by a blaster bolt, seemingly in the crossfire of a local street gang fight, and her entire left arm was severed. Windu attempted to save her by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to pinch the brachial artery shut, but he was shot by the Balawai militia. Tenk promptly died of [[Blood/Legends|blood]] loss.

Windu would later learn of Tenk's treachery after he confronted the "upcountry team," which was merely a band of thugs on Tenk's payroll that were to assassinate Windu. He would also learn that she had been hit not by a misfired shot from a gang sniper but by [[Nick Rostu]], who had been sent by Master Billaba for Windu and was aware of Tenk's [[Treason/Legends|treason]].
